To find the values of $ a $ and $ b $ that make the surface area of the given parametric surface equal to 1000 square units, we first compute the surface area using the formula for the surface area of a parametric surface:

$$
A = \iint_D \left\| \frac{\partial \mathbf{r}}{\partial u} \times \frac{\partial \mathbf{r}}{\partial v} \right\| \, du \, dv
$$

Where $ \mathbf{r}(u, v) = (a \sin(u) \cos(v), a \sin(u) \sin(v), b \cos(u)) $.

### Step 1: Compute the partial derivatives

$$
\frac{\partial \mathbf{r}}{\partial u} = \left( a \cos(u) \cos(v), a \cos(u) \sin(v), -b \sin(u) \right)
$$

$$
\frac{\partial \mathbf{r}}{\partial v} = \left( -a \sin(u) \sin(v), a \sin(u) \cos(v), 0 \right)
$$

### Step 2: Compute the cross product

$$
\frac{\partial \mathbf{r}}{\partial u} \times \frac{\partial \mathbf{r}}{\partial v} =
\begin{vmatrix}
\mathbf{i} & \mathbf{j} & \mathbf{k} \\
a \cos(u) \cos(v) & a \cos(u) \sin(v) & -b \sin(u) \\
-a \sin(u) \sin(v) & a \sin(u) \cos(v) & 0 \\
\end{vmatrix}
$$

$$
= \left( a \cos(u) \sin(u) \cos(v) \right) \mathbf{i} - \left( a \cos(u) \sin(u) \sin(v) \right) \mathbf{j} + \left( a \sin(u) \sin(u) \sin(v) \right) \mathbf{k}
$$
